name: Akaunting
version_status: CONFIRMED enough for research / release cadence should be rechecked at implementation
what: Open-source-style accounting platform covering bookkeeping, invoicing, expenses, banking, and app/extensions ecosystem.
solves: General small-business accounting under self-host control, broader than pure invoicing.
users: Small businesses seeking a self-hostable accounting app.
fit: Lower fit than Invoice Ninja for Hylono’s near-term stack. It is broader accounting software and more likely to overlap with existing finance tooling rather than directly serving product integration needs.
runtime: PHP/Laravel-style accounting web app with modules, DB, mail, and finance workflows.
components: Web UI/API, database, accounting modules, extensions, import/export, and document generation.
deploy: Standard web-app self-host patterns; use only if Hylono truly wants an accounting system rather than invoicing adjunct.
deps: Database, mailer, storage, cron/jobs, finance process ownership.
auth: Local user roles; verify SSO options if needed.
apis: Accounting/invoice/bank-related app surfaces; deeper API suitability should be tested against current version.
security: Financial and accounting records create governance obligations beyond ordinary app data.
setup_path: Do not deploy speculatively. First confirm that an accounting platform belongs in scope and that BSL licensing is acceptable.
env: App URL, DB settings, app key, mail, storage, cron, and extension config.
persistence: Back up DB and generated financial documents; preserve chart-of-accounts and fiscal settings carefully.
operations: Version, extension, and fiscal-rule changes require finance stakeholder oversight.
failure_points: Using it where invoicing-only tooling or external accounting software would be better, and introducing license friction late.
integration: Could integrate with Stripe, Invoice Ninja, n8n, and reporting, but is not a strong first integration target for the public Hylono stack.
verify: Confirm whether Hylono already has accounting software. If yes, Akaunting is probably redundant.
recommendation: LIKELY EXCLUDE / ONLY IF ACCOUNTING GAP EXISTS
overlap: Substantial overlap with Invoice Ninja and external accounting suites.
codex_read_first: Read the overlap map before doing any Akaunting work.
codex_best_path: Do not implement unless a real accounting-system gap is confirmed.
codex_never_assume: Never assume a self-hosted accounting system is worth the operational burden when invoicing needs can be met elsewhere.

assets.name: Official site
assets.notes: Product positioning and docs entry point.
